Japanese: 同日　午後　１時１４分 地方裁判所　被告人第４控え室
English: February 16, 1:14 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 4

Japanese: 尾並田さ
English: Mr. Fawles, I...

Japanese: うがーーーー！
English: UGGAAAA!

Japanese: きゃああ！ ゴメンなさいゴメンなさい！
English: Eeeeeek! I'm sorry! I-I'm sorry!

Japanese: オレ、モーレツにカンドー。 アンタ、恩人。
English: I wanna say thanks. You're real good! You really hooked me up!

Japanese: あと、１歩です！ 彼女の犯行を立証できれば‥‥
English: Thanks! We're almost there! Once I prove that she committed the crime...

Japanese: ‥‥そこに、もう１つのカベが 立ちふさがるだろうぜ。
English: ...Yeah, but there's one more big obstacle we've got to get past.

Japanese: か、“カベ”‥‥ですか？
English: Ob-Obstacle...?

Japanese: 無久井　里子が‥‥なぜ。 あの婦人警官を殺害したのか‥‥？
English: Yeah. Motive. Why would Melissa Foster kill that policewoman anyway?
(Motive, huh...)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥オレたちには、 致命的に情報が足りねえ。
English: Anyway... We're still badly in need of information.

Japanese: 情報‥‥‥
English: Information... Right...

Japanese: まず、足りねえのは 無久井　里子に関する情報だ。
English: What we need the most is info about this Melissa Foster herself.

Japanese: “文学部の学生” オレたちは、それしか知らねえ。
English: All we know is that she's a student studying literature.

Japanese: そして‥‥もう１つ。
English: And one more thing.

Japanese: ‥‥なんですか！
English: ...What is it?

Japanese: もちろん‥‥５年前の事件だ。
English: Well, the incident that happened 5 years ago, of course.

Japanese: このシマシマが死刑判決を 受けた、例の誘拐殺人事件さ。
English: The kidnapping-murder case that Zebra Boy is on death row for...

Japanese: オレ！　なんにもしてない！ オレ！　ウソ、つかない！
English: I din't do nuttin'! I din't kill nobody! I never lie!

Japanese: 尾並田さん！　それならば‥‥ 私たちに、話してくれますか？
English: Mr. Fawles! In that case... Tell us more about it.

Japanese: ‥‥５年前の事件のこと。
English: ...About what happened 5 years ago.

Japanese: わかった。‥‥オレ。 アンタ、信じる。
English: OK. I trust you.

Japanese: ５年間‥‥毎日。ユメ、見る。 ‥‥あの日のコト‥‥
English: That day. 5 years ago. I dream of it. Every day.

Japanese: この写真‥‥ナツカシイ。
English: This picture. It reminds me everything.

Japanese: おぼろ橋、変わってない。 ５年前も、こんな感じ‥‥
English: Bridge looks same. Just like then... 5 years ago...

Japanese: ‥‥ボロボロに壊れて、 今にもくずれそうだった。
English: Like it could fall apart... Fall apart any minute...

Japanese: （５年前から壊れていたんだ‥‥ この吊り橋‥‥）
English: (So it's been broken like that for at least 5 years...)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　ボロボロでくずれそう なのは、おたがいサマだがな。
English: Ha...! Sorry buddy, but you sound like the one that could fall apart at any minute.

Japanese: ‥‥オレ。オレ‥‥ たしかにユーカイ、した。
English: It true. I did... ...I did kidnap her.

Japanese: ‥‥５年前。オレのコイビト‥‥ 美柳　ちなみを。
English: ...Five years ago. I kidnapped. My girlfriend. Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: こここ、コイビト‥‥？
English: Y-Your g-girlfriend? Huh...?

Japanese: ‥‥おい！　ちょっと待てよ。 “みやなぎ”だと‥‥？
English: Hey, hold on there! Did you say, "Hawthorne"!?

Japanese: （被害者と‥‥同じ名字！）
English: (The victim's last name...!)

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみ‥‥イモウト。 ‥‥美柳　勇希の。
English: Dahlia Hawthorne... Valerie's little sister.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: Wh-Wh-Whaaat!? Are you serious!?

Japanese: その子を‥‥人質をはなしなさい！
English: The girl... Let her go!

Japanese: ダマれ！　ち、近寄ると‥‥ ヒトジチ、殺すッ！
English: Shut up! C-Come closer... And I kill her!

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンだったわね。 あなたは、ここまでよ‥‥。
English: Sorry, but you're not going to get the chance...

Japanese: （あのときの刑事が‥‥ 美柳　勇希だった）
English: (The detective back then was Valerie Hawthorne!)

Japanese: 人質をとった犯人に発砲なんて、 やりすぎだと思ってたけど‥‥
English: At first I thought shooting someone for a kidnapping was crossing the line, but...

Japanese: 妹さんを助けるため だったんですね‥‥
English: If it was to protect her little sister, I can understand why she did it...

Japanese: ちがう！　あのオンナ‥‥勇希！ 裏切った！　オレたちを！
English: Wrong! No protect sister! Valerie betray me! Betray us!

Japanese: “裏切った”って‥‥ どういうことですか！
English: What do you mean she betrayed you!?

Japanese: ゼンブ。‥‥ゼンブ‥‥ 狂言だった。‥‥ユーカイ。
English: Everything. All lies. All make-believe. Kidnapping, too...
A make-believe kidnapping?

Japanese: 彼女‥‥ちなみ。 オレの、天使。宝物だった。
English: Dahlia. My girlfriend. My love. My Teen Angel.

Japanese: （いまどき“テンシ”と 来たか‥‥）
English: (Ugh. Did he actually say, "My Teen Angel"? He's seen one too many soap operas.)

Japanese: アイツの言うコト、なんでもやる。 ちなみに言われたコトだったら‥‥
English: I do anything she says. Anything Dahlia says...

Japanese: （“ちなみに言われた”‥‥？） ちょ、ちょっと待ってください！
English: ("Anything Dahlia says"...?) H-Hold on a minute!

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥５年前の 誘拐を計画したのは‥‥？
English: What you're saying is that the kidnapping 5 years ago was planned by...

Japanese: オレと、ちなみ。 ‥‥勇希も、仲間だった。
English: Yeah. Me and Dahlia... ...And Valerie, too.

Japanese: （勇希さんも‥‥！）
English: (Valerie was in on it!?)

Japanese: ちなみの家‥‥宝石商。金持ち。 宝石の１個ぐらい‥‥そう思った。
English: Dahlia's family rich. Jewelry business. We get one jewel... That's what we thought...

Japanese: 脅迫状、ちなみとオレが書いた。 ‥‥彼女の父親に。
English: Me and Dahlia wrote kidnap note. We send to her dad.

Japanese: 要求は、２億円相当のダイヤ。 受け渡し場所はおぼろ橋の上。
English: Asked for 2 million dollar diamond. Tell him make exchange on Dusky Bridge.

Japanese: ‥‥取引相手が、美柳　勇希。 刑事になったばかりだった。
English: ...We tell him Valerie make transfer 'cause she new detective.

Japanese: 刑事が仲間にいれば、たしかに 有利だっただろうぜ。
English: Having a police detective in your pocket is a useful thing alright.

Japanese: その３人で、２億を山わけする はずだった、ってワケか‥‥
English: In the end, you were planning on splitting the 2 million three ways, huh...

Japanese: ‥‥でも！　あのオンナ‥‥！
English: Yeah, but! That woman!

Japanese: 美柳　勇希‥‥アイツ、 ホンキだった。
English: That woman! Valerie! She do it for real!

Japanese: ホンキで‥‥オレを撃った！ オレと、ちなみを‥‥
English: She shoot at me for real! Me and Dahlia!

Japanese: パァァァン！
English: *BANG!*

Japanese: オレ。腕を撃たれた。そして ちなみは‥‥川に身を投げた。
English: I was shot in arm. Dahlia... She jump in river.

Japanese: “身を投げた”‥‥って、 自分から飛びおりたんですか！
English: Jump...? You don't mean she jumped on purpose, do you?

Japanese: ‥‥オレにはできない！ ちなみ、つき落とすなんて‥‥
English: I couldn't do it! I could never push her...!

Japanese: オレ。ボーゼンとして‥‥ 気がついたらタイホされてた。
English: Anyway, I blacked out... Wake up with police all over.

Japanese: それで‥‥死刑判決を 受けたワケか‥‥
English: ...And that's when they decided to give you the death sentence.

Japanese: 信じられなかった。あのオンナ‥‥ オレを裏切るなんて！
English: I couldn't believe it. That woman. She betrayed me.

Japanese: あのオトコは‥‥尾並田は 突きおとしたんです！　私の妹を！
English: That man... Terry Fawles... He killed her! He threw her off the bridge!

Japanese: １２メートルも下の、急流に！ 私の目の前で、ちなみを‥‥！
English: He threw my beloved sister into the roaring river 40 feet below!

Japanese: ‥‥この５年間。オレ。死ぬ前に、 どうしても聞きたかった。
English: ...These 5 years... All I wonder is... Why! ...Why! ...Why! ...Why!

Japanese: なぜ‥‥どうして、あんなウソを！ それだけ、ずっと考えていた。
English: Why did she lie!? That's all I want to know!

Japanese: だから、美柳　勇希を‥‥ 呼びだした。‥‥おぼろ橋に。
English: So that's why you called her. You wanted to hear the truth from Valerie herself.

Japanese: ‥‥アイツのカオ、忘れていた。 マフラー、つけさせた。
English: Yes... but I forget what she look like. So I tell her to wear scarf.

Japanese: 殺すつもり、なかった！ ‥‥ただ、聞きたかった。
English: I don't want to hurt her! ...Just ask why!

Japanese: どうして、ウソの証言をした‥‥？ どうして、オレを裏切った‥‥？
English: Why! Why did you lie...? Why did you betray me...?

Japanese: ‥‥どうしても聞きたかった。 彼女自身のクチから。
English: I just want to hear answer come from her mouth. That's all!

Japanese: （だから‥‥ 望みのない脱走を‥‥）
English: (So that's why... That's why you made a crazy escape like that...)

Japanese: すまねえが‥‥シマシマさんよぉ。
English: Just one thing, Zebra Boy...

Japanese: オレはビンボー症でな。 気になることがあるんだ。
English: My senses are tingling all over. Tell me, Mr. Fawles... Where is it?

Japanese: ‥‥なんですか？
English: ...Huh? Where's what?

Japanese: 決まってるだろう。 身代金‥‥２億円のダイヤさ。
English: Come on now, Kitten... The ransom. The 2 million dollar diamond.

Japanese: アレはどうなったんだ？ オヤジさんに返したのかい？
English: Remember that now? Did you give it back to Pops? Did the police take it?

Japanese: ‥‥知らない。
English: ...I dunno.

Japanese: え。知らない‥‥？ また、そんな‥‥
English: Huh? You don't know?

Japanese: オレ。ホントに知らない。 ちなみといっしょに、消えた。
English: No really. I dunno. It's gone. With Dahlia.

Japanese: ‥‥ちなみさん‥‥？
English: ...With Dahlia?

Japanese: あの日、橋の上‥‥
English: That day. On the bridge...

Japanese: 身代金のダイヤ‥‥ちなみの リュックサックに入っていた。
English: Dahlia put it. In backpack...

Japanese: ‥‥彼女といっしょに‥‥ 橋の上から、消えた。
English: Now gone. With Dahlia. Gone... Forever...

Japanese: 吾童川の、急流にのまれて‥‥
English: Into Eagle River...

Japanese: （ダイヤが“消えた”‥‥？ ちょっと、待ってよ‥‥）
English: (It disappeared with Dahlia, huh...? Wait a minute...!)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人！　被告人！ そろそろ審理が再開します。
English: ...You can come back in now! We're about ready to go!

Japanese: 尾並田さん！ 最後に、１つだけ！
English: Mr. Fawles! Just one more question!

Japanese: ‥‥“彼女といっしょに” ダイヤが消えた、ということは‥‥
English: When you said, "with Dahlia", do you mean the diamond is still missing...?

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみさんの死体は？ どうなったのかしら。
English: Along with the body of Dahlia Hawthorne?

Japanese: 見つかっていない‥‥。 今も、まだ。
English: Never found her... My sweet Dahlia...

Japanese: （見つかっていない‥‥！）
English: (They never found her...!)

Japanese: 急流にのまれて‥‥消えたまま。 ‥‥ちなみ‥‥オレの、天使‥‥
English: Swallowed by river... Gone... Dahlia... My Teen Angel...

Japanese: あんたの“天使”‥‥ いったい、いくつだったんだい？
English: Your "Teen Angel"...? How old was she anyway?
Just 14...

Japanese: じゅ‥‥じゅうよんさい！ （子どもじゃないの！）
English: F-F-Fourteen!? (I guess you were robbing cradles before diamonds...)

Japanese: 狂言誘拐を計画して、２億円の ダイヤを抱いて急流に消えた‥‥
English: She plans a fake kidnapping and disappears into the river with a rock worth 2 mil...

Japanese: まったく‥‥最近の天使は おマセさんだぜ。
English: Man oh man... Angels these days.

Japanese: （‥‥そして、尾並田さんは 死刑判決を受けた‥‥）
English: (Fawles takes the fall... And gets a one-way ticket to Death Row...)

Japanese: （“美柳　ちなみ”‥‥ 天使なのか、それとも‥‥）
English: (Is Dahlia Hawthorne an angel or is she really a...)

Japanese: ‥‥時間だ、コネコちゃん。 どうやら、武器を手に入れたな。
English: ...It's time, Kitten. It looks like we have a few more aces up our sleeve now.

Japanese: ‥‥はい！
English: ...You bet!

Japanese: 証拠品《ダイヤモンド》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Diamond added to the Court Record.

Japanese: エンリョすることはねえ。 パンチは、打てるときに打て！
English: The training wheels come off now, Mia! You've got to strike while the iron is hot!

Japanese: ‥‥そいつが、オレのルールだぜ。
English: That's one of my rules. Remember it.

Japanese: 同日　午後　１時４９分 地方裁判所　第４法廷
English: February 16, 1:49 PM District Court Courtroom No. 4

Japanese: それでは、尾並田　美散の審理を しめやかに再開いたしましょう。
English: Now then, let's continue with the trial of Mr. Terry Fawles.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。もう大丈夫ですか？
English: ...Witness. Are you feeling better?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥すみません。 わたし、がんばりますね。
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor... I-I'll try my best.

Japanese: ぬうう‥‥。 ケナゲですな、まったく。
English: Hmm... You're a brave young lady.

Japanese: （まだ言ってる‥‥）
English: (Not this again...)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人が、依頼人を助けたい 気持ちはリカイできる。
English: I can understand a defense lawyer wanting to get her client off the hook...

Japanese: しかし！　その罪を、なんの関係も ない証人に着せようとは‥‥
English: However! To try to pin the crime on an innocent student is...

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか！
English: What are you talking about!?

Japanese: この証人は、あらゆるイミで、 この事件と接点がない。
English: My witness is not the person on trial here.

Japanese: たまたま吾童山中で事件を目撃した ‥‥それだけのコトだ。
English: She's an innocent bystander who witnessed a violent crime. ...That's all.

Japanese: そんな彼女が、なぜ！　婦人警官を 殺害しなければならないのか！
English: What possible reason would a girl like this have for murdering a policewoman!

Japanese: ぬううう‥‥ たしかに、動機がありません。
English: Hmm... It's certainly hard to imagine this woman as a murderer...

Japanese: （“動機”‥‥やっぱり それが次の問題、よね‥‥）
English: (Her motive, huh... I figured that's what I had to establish next...)

Japanese: いかがですか？　弁護人。 立証できるのでしょうな？
English: Well, Ms. Fey? Do you have any evidence of a motive?

Japanese: あ、モチロンです！　‥‥たぶん。
English: Err, yes! Of course! ...I think.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　だから コネコちゃんなんだよ、アンタは。
English: Hah...! You're still acting as tame as a kitten... Kitten.

Japanese: 神乃木さん‥‥
English: Mr. Armando...

Japanese: 弁護士はなァ。ピンチのときほど、 ふてぶてしく笑うモンだぜ。
English: Listen... A lawyer is someone who smiles no matter how bad it gets.

Japanese: この状況で、思わずニヤけちまう ヤツこそが、“プロ”なのさ‥‥。
English: Smiling on the outside while your guts are twisted in knots is the mark of a pro.

Japanese: ‥‥その、うれしそうな ニヤニヤ笑い、やめてください。
English: ...Maybe so, but I wish you would quit grinning at me like that.

Japanese: あの‥‥ちょっと、 よろしいですか？　オジサマ。
English: Umm... Excuse me... May I speak, Mr. Judge?

Japanese: ぬ！　オジサマは、 いつでもよろしいですぞ！
English: Of course! Mr. Judge is ready anytime you like!

Japanese: 証言‥‥させて いただきたいんです。
English: I'd like... I'd like to say something.

Japanese: わたし、疑われているのですよね？ ですから、それが‥‥
English: Some people here are suspicious of me, right? Th-That's why...

Japanese: ヒドいぬれぎぬだって、オジサマ だけには、わかってほしくて。
English: I... I at least wanted you, Mr. Judge, to know that it's not true...

Japanese: ぬうううう！ そうだったのですかっ！
English: Hmmmmm! I see! You're such an honest and upstanding young lady.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、あの証人のほうが “プロ”らしいな。
English: It looks like this witness is a real professional.

Japanese: ど、どうしてですか！
English: Wh-What do you mean!?

Japanese: 見ろよ、あのまぶしい笑顔をよォ。
English: Look at that 100-watt smile.

Japanese: ‥‥とびっきりピンチなのさ。 あのカワイコちゃんも、な‥‥。
English: ...Just when things are darkest for her... CLICK. She lights right up!

Japanese: それでは！　証人の話を 聞くことにしましょう！
English: Very well then! Let's hear what the witness has to say!

Japanese: ～証人自身について～
English: -- Melissa Foster's History --

Japanese: 私‥‥おととしまで、ずっと この国にいなかったんです。
English: I... I was out of the country until the year before last.

Japanese: 大学に入るまで、吾童山にも 行ったことはありません。
English: Until I entered college, I had never even been to Eagle Mountain before...

Japanese: わたしに、お動機なんて あるハズがありませんのよ。
English: And I certainly don't have any reason for wanting to hurt a police officer.

Japanese: ５年前、自分を逮捕した刑事さまを 逆恨みして、殺害なさるなんて‥‥
English: Holding a grudge and killing the officer who testified against you 5 years ago...

Japanese: ユーカイまでなさっているのに。 被告人さま、ヒドいひとです！
English: Or kidnapping a poor girl... I just think the defendant is a terrible, horrible monster!

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥ この国にいなかった‥‥
English: Hmm... Out of the country, eh...

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: Precisely.

Japanese: ‥‥殺人の動機など、 持ちようがないではないか。
English: Furthermore, she has no possible motive for committing murder.

Japanese: うぬううう‥‥
English: Hmm, indeed...

Japanese: ‥‥さてと。 ニッコリ行くぜ、コネコちゃん。
English: You're up to bat, Kitten. Sharpen those claws and put on your best smile.

Japanese: は‥‥はい！
English: Y-You bet!

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥彼女と事件を なんとかして結びつけるの‥‥）
English: (Somehow, I have to tie her to this case...)

Japanese: ～証人自身について～
English: -- Melissa Foster's History --

Japanese: 私‥‥おととしまで、ずっと この国にいなかったんです。
English: I... I was out of the country until the year before last.

Japanese: 大学に入るまで、吾童山にも 行ったことはありません。
English: Until I entered college, I had never even been to Eagle Mountain before...

Japanese: わたしに、お動機なんて あるハズがありませんのよ。
English: And I certainly don't have any reason for wanting to hurt a police officer.

Japanese: ５年前、自分を逮捕した刑事さまを 逆恨みして、殺害なさるなんて‥‥
English: Holding a grudge and killing the officer who testified against you 5 years ago...

Japanese: ユーカイまでなさっているのに。 被告人さま、ヒドいひとです！
English: Or kidnapping a poor girl... I just think the defendant is a terrible, horrible monster!

Japanese: （こんなテキトーな証言で 逃がすワケには行かない‥‥！）
English: (I can't let her get away with these lies...!)

Japanese: 彼女はかならず、この事件に 深く関わっているはずだ。
English: Listen to me. She's neck-deep in this whole thing...

Japanese: あのカワイコちゃんの “正体”‥‥暴いてやろうぜ。
English: Somehow, you're just going to have to get her to show the court her true self.

Japanese: だから‥‥当然、被害者も 犯人のかたも、知りませんの。
English: Naturally, I didn't know either the victim or the defendant.

Japanese: 白いマフラーを持っていなくて わたし、ラッキーでしたのね。
English: I guess I'm lucky I wasn't wearing a white scarf.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥どの国にいたんですか？
English: So, what country were you living in then?

Japanese: あの、わたし‥‥。 帰国子女なんです。
English: We were all living abroad, but after my parents were killed...

Japanese: 内乱の激しい国で両親を失い、 ひとりで帰国されたと聞いている。
English: It was a brutal civil war. She had to try to make her way back home alone...

Japanese: だから‥‥身分を証明できる ものが、１つもなくて‥‥。
English: I lost everything... I didn't even have any personal identification...

Japanese: （なんてテキトーな 身の上なの‥‥）
English: (What kind of sob story is this...?)

Japanese: （‥‥どうしよう。 詳しく聞いたほうがいいかな？）
English: (...What do I do? Should I press her for details?)

Japanese: ‥‥証人。 質問に答えてください。
English: ...Witness. Answer my question.

Japanese: 『どこの国にいたのか？』 ハッキリさせましょう。
English: I'll even repeat it for you. What country were you in?

Japanese: ‥‥証人がどこの国にいて、 何カ国語をあやつろうが‥‥
English: Your Honor, this line of questioning is childish.

Japanese: この審理には関係ない。 重要なのは‥‥
English: What country she was in and how many languages she may speak are irrelevant here.

Japanese: ‥‥証人が、事件と いかなる関係にあるか‥‥？
English: What we're here to evaluate is whether this witness has any connection to this case.

Japanese: わたし、おさないころから 外国生活でしたから‥‥
English: I've lived abroad ever since I was a little girl...

Japanese: 尾並田さまも、美柳刑事さまも 存じあげませんわ。
English: That's why I could never have known Mr. Fawles or Detective Hawthorne.

Japanese: そう‥‥。それが、 ただ１つのポイントなのだ‥‥
English: Yes... I think we've established that point.

Japanese: そのとおりですな。
English: Yes, indeed.

Japanese: では、証人。今の発言を 証言に加えてもらえますかな？
English: Well then. Shall we add what you've just stated to the official testimony?

Japanese: もちろん、よろこんで。 ‥‥オジサマ。
English: Yes, please. ...Mr. Judge.

Japanese: （“身分を証明できない”と 言われては、どうしようもない）
English: (There's nothing I can do about her claim to have lost all her identification.)

Japanese: （彼女の正体を立証できる証拠が 見つかるまで待つの、千尋！）
English: (All I can do is wait to find some other evidence about her real identity.)

Japanese: それでは、証人。 つづけてもらいましょうか。
English: Very well then, Ms. Foster. Please proceed.

Japanese: そもそも、どうして 吾童山へ行ったのですか？
English: So what made you decide to go to Eagle Mountain anyway?

Japanese: わたし、ハイキングと ピクニックが大好きですの。
English: I just love being outdoors... Picnics, hiking... You know, that sort of thing.

Japanese: （どうちがうのかしら？ その２つは‥‥）
English: (...You don't look like much of a hiker to me. But you do look like a digger of sorts.)

Japanese: 吾童山は、この街からクルマで ２時間、電車も通っていません。
English: But Eagle Mountain is a two-hour drive from here and no trains run through there.

Japanese: もっと近くて便利な山が いくらでもあるのに‥‥
English: There are plenty of mountains that are closer and easier to get to.

Japanese: 大学の《さんぽ同好会》で一度、 みなさんと参りましたの。
English: Well, I went there once with the college Hiking Club.

Japanese: 荒れはてて打ちひしがれた、 うすらサムい感じが気に入って‥‥
English: I fell in love with its stark, desolate beauty and its cold, yet romantic, gloominess...

Japanese: （変わったシュミね‥‥）
English: (Didn't know you were such a goth...)

Japanese: ところで、大学というのは、 どちらの‥‥？
English: By the way, what's the name of your college...?

Japanese: 証人の身分に関する質問は、 検察側はいっさい認めない。
English: The prosecution objects to any questions that involve the witness's private life.

Japanese: かんじんなのは、彼女が 『事件を目撃したこと』のみ。
English: All that matters is that she is a material witness to a crime.

Japanese: 非道なる弁護人の悪意にみちた 質問に、答える必要はない。
English: The witness doesn't need to respond to questions that are clearly malicious in intent.

Japanese: そうです！ あんまりですわ‥‥
English: Thank you! She's really gone too far...!

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥。 弁護人！　ユルしませんぞ！
English: Hmmmmm! Ms. Fey! You're treading on thin ice here!

Japanese: （そんなにヒドいコトを 聞いたのかしら、私‥‥）
English: (I hardly said anything! Talk about sensitive...)

Japanese: でも！　あなたの事件当日の 行動は、かなり疑わしい！
English: Perhaps, but your behavior that day was very suspicious!

Japanese: 証言はムジュンしているし、 犯人しか知らない情報‥‥
English: Not only have you contradicted yourself here in court, but you know things you shouldn't.

Japanese: トランクのキズのことも 知っていました。
English: For example, the scratches on the trunk of the car.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: Well th-that's...

Japanese: ザンネンながら、弁護士クン。 それはなんの証拠にもならない。
English: Unfortunately Ms. Fey, your last statement proves nothing.

Japanese: なぜですか！
English: Oh really!? And why is that?

Japanese: 証人には、被害者の特定のため 一度、検事局に来てもらっている。
English: The witness came to the police station once to identify the suspect.

Japanese: そのとき‥‥この写真を、係官が 見せた可能性があるのでね。
English: It's entirely possible that, at that time, an officer showed her this photo.

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥。 それは、あるまじきミスですな。
English: Hmm... That seems like a rather serious mistake.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　出たぜ。 検察側、お得意の戦法だ。
English: Ha...! That's the oldest trick in the prosecutor's book.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥！
English: Th-That's not fair...!

Japanese: わたし‥‥ユルせないんです。 あの日見た、囚人服のかた‥‥
English: That wicked inmate... I'll never be able to forget that horrible day...

Japanese: “さかうらみ”‥‥？
English: A grudge...?

Japanese: あの。被害者のカタの証言が キメ手になったのですよね？
English: Well, the policewoman's testimony was crucial, wasn't it?

Japanese: 被告人さまの‥‥ 死刑判決の、キメ手に。
English: ...Crucial in getting the defendant sentenced to death.

Japanese: そのとおり。被告人は、 それをウラんでいたのだ。
English: Yes, and that's precisely why he harbored such deep anger against her.

Japanese: ‥‥自分の罪を棚に上げて‥‥
English: So much anger that he forgot his own guilt...

Japanese: 尾並田さんは、５年前の罪を 認めていません！
English: My client has always maintained that he's innocent of those charges!

Japanese: ずいぶん、忘れっぽいのですね。 ‥‥被告人さまは。
English: He seems rather forgetful. ...Your client, I mean.

Japanese: 刑事さまのコトはおろか、ご自分の なさったことまでお忘れなんて。
English: Not only did he forget about what he did, but he forgot the poor policewoman as well.

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean by that?

Japanese: 被告人さま‥‥刑事さまの おカオ、お忘れだったのでしょう？
English: Your client... He forgot what the detective looked like, right?

Japanese: それなら、いっそ‥‥証言のことも 忘れてしまえばよろしかったのに。
English: It's too bad for her that he didn't forget about her testimony as well.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥尾並田さん、 忘れっぽいみたいだけど‥‥）
English: (Well, she's right about that. Mr. Fawles is kind of forgetful...)

Japanese: “被害者のカオを忘れていた” ‥‥というのは？
English: You said, "he forgot what the detective looked like." What did you mean by that?

Japanese: だって、目印がなければ、ご本人と わからなかったのですよね？
English: Well he couldn't tell who she was without some kind of identification, right?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥被害者は現場で、 目印のマフラーをしていた。
English: Quite right... That's why the victim was wearing a scarf as identification.

Japanese: ‥‥もし、あの日。 白いマフラーをしていたら‥‥
English: Why, if I had been wearing a white scarf that day...

Japanese: わたし、あのかたに狙われたかも しれないのですよね‥‥
English: ...then he probably would have tried to kill me...

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥たしかに。 ユルしがたいことです！
English: Hmm... That's true. He's clearly a bitter man!

Japanese: （マズいわ。尾並田さんの印象が どんどん悪くなっていく‥‥）
English: (This is bad. Mr. Fawles's reputation just keeps getting worse and worse.)

Japanese: （深追いはキケンだけど、 今の発言‥‥どうかしら？）
English: (Sometimes it's best not to poke too deep... What should I do with that last statement?)

Japanese: （これ以上、尾並田さんのウッカリ ぶりが強調されちゃったら‥‥）
English: (Any more damage to my client's image could be a disaster...)

Japanese: （裁判長に、よけい悪い印象を 与えかねないわ‥‥）
English: (The judge already has a bad enough opinion of him...)

Japanese: 忘れっぽい人間は、 信用できませんね。
English: You just can't trust someone with a bad memory.

Japanese: １週間前の今日、何を食べたか？
English: What did you eat for dinner one week ago today?

Japanese: ‥‥最低でも、それぐらいは 押さえておきたいものです。
English: ...At the very least, a person should remember that much.

Japanese: ちなみに私は、給料日だったので 家族でスキヤキをいただきました。
English: As for me, it was payday, so I had a nice moose steak with some poutine on the side.

Japanese: （‥‥だから何よ！）
English: (...And how does this relate to anything!?)

Japanese: スキヤキにしろ、犯罪にしろ、 自らの行いを否定してはいけない。
English: Whether it's eating steak or committing murder, a person shouldn't deny their actions.

Japanese: ‥‥そういうことだ。
English: I'm in complete agreement, Your Honor.

Japanese: 裁判長！　ただ今の証人の 発言‥‥非常に重要です！
English: Your Honor! What the witness said just now was tremendously important!

Japanese: 証言に加えていただけますか！
English: I'd like it added to the official testimony!

Japanese: 検察側にも、異議はない‥‥
English: The prosecution has no objection...

Japanese: 被告人が、見さかいなく殺意を バラまく、危険人物だったこと‥‥
English: After all, the defendant is a killer and a mentally unbalanced one at that...

Japanese: それを立証する、貴重な証言 だからな‥‥
English: That testimony only helps to further prove that point...

Japanese: ぬうおおおお‥‥！
English: Hmm...!

Japanese: い、いえ！ そういうつもりでは‥‥
English: N-No, that's not why I...

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥証人。おねがいします。
English: Enough. ...Witness, if you would?

Japanese: もちろん、よろこんで。
English: My pleasure, Mr. Judge.

Japanese: この事件のことは 知っていたのですか？
English: You knew about that incident?

Japanese: おととしまで、この国には いなかったんですよね？
English: But weren't you out of the country until the year before last?

Japanese: 脱走なさった死刑囚さまのこと、 ニュースで拝見したものですから。
English: Well, I saw a report about the escaped convict on the news.

Japanese: わたし流に調べてみましたの。 ワイドショーなどを参考に。
English: They had an in-depth report about his whole history.

Japanese: ５年前、あなたはまだ、 外国にいらっしゃったワケですな？
English: So you were still living abroad 5 years ago, is that right?

Japanese: ええ‥‥。
English: Yes...

Japanese: “ふたりを知らない”‥‥ まちがいありませんね？
English: You didn't know either person? Are you certain of that?

Japanese: ええ。わたし、 人見知りするほうですから。
English: Yes. I'm afraid I'm rather shy around people.

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥ それは、しかたないですねえ。
English: Hmm... Oh well, that can't be helped.

Japanese: （イミがわからない‥‥）
English: (Why is he just agreeing with everything that comes out of her mouth...?)

Japanese: 吾童山の吊り橋の上で、初めて ふたりを見たわけですか？
English: The first time you saw either of them was when they were on the bridge, correct?

Japanese: ええ‥‥。本当に、 グーゼンだったんです。
English: Yes... It really was a coincidence.

Japanese: “ラッキーだった” というのは‥‥？
English: What do you mean by lucky?

Japanese: だって‥‥今は２月ですから。 みんなマフラーをしています。
English: Well... It's February now. Everyone is wearing scarves.

Japanese: もしグーゼンに、指定どおりの 白いマフラーをしていたら‥‥
English: If I had accidentally worn a white scarf like he said...

Japanese: 証人自身が、キケンに さらされたかもしれない。
English: Then you yourself might have been killed.

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥ッ！
English: Hmmmmm... That would've been a terrible loss for this world!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　今のゆさぶりは よけいだったぜ‥‥
English: Ha...! It looks like you pressed too hard this time, Kitten.

Japanese: 神乃木さん‥‥
English: Mr. Armando...

Japanese: ようすを見ているうちは、 ゴールは目に入らねえ。
English: Keep looking around you and you're going to lose sight of the finish line.

Japanese: 早いトコ、つかまえないと‥‥ 法廷の女神、そっぽ向いちゃうぜ！
English: Justice is blind, but she's not deaf. Sometimes you have to know when not to talk.

Japanese: 証人。‥‥あなたが撮影した、 この写真を見てください。
English: Witness... I want you to look at this photo you took.

Japanese: この写真では、ハッキリしません。 被害者の目印である、マフラー‥‥
English: It's hard to see in the photo, but look at the scarf the victim wore as identification.

Japanese: マフラーというのは、モチロン。 これのコトでしょうな？
English: Ah, you are talking about this scarf right here, eh?

Japanese: そ、そう！　それですわ！ 刑事さまがしていた、マフラー‥‥
English: Y-Yes! That's it! The scarf the policewoman was wearing...

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥つかまえたようね）
English: (I've got her now, just don't mess up...)

Japanese: ‥‥それは、ヘンですね。
English: ...But that's strange.

Japanese: あなたは、たった今、 こう証言したはずよ。
English: In your testimony, you stated the following...

Japanese: 《白いマフラーを持っていなくて よかった》、と‥‥
English: "I guess I'm lucky I wasn't wearing a white scarf."
Wh-White?

Japanese: ‥‥これが、あなたも認めた 被害者のマフラーです。
English: ...This is the scarf you identified as belonging to the victim.

Japanese: どこからどう見ても、 《白》ではありません！
English: But it certainly doesn't look white to me!

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！
事件当日、現場は小雨がふって キリも出ていた！
English: Well, it was foggy that day... And it was raining as well!

Japanese: 被告人が見まちがえても、 ムリはない！
English: It's not surprising that she mistook it for white!

Japanese: そうは行きません。
English: Sorry, but not this time.

Japanese: 証人はたった今、認めたのです。 これが被害者のマフラーだと！
English: The witness just confirmed that this was the victim's scarf!

Japanese: しかし‥‥いったい なぜ、そんなカンちがいを‥‥？
English: Yes, but... What's the significance...?

Japanese: たしかに、このマフラーは どう見ても、白ではない‥‥
English: It's true that the scarf doesn't look white, but...

Japanese: その理由は‥‥ １つしか考えられません！
English: There's only one explanation for this mix-up!

Japanese: 証人がマフラーの色を 《白》だとカンちがいした理由は！
English: The reason why the witness thought the scarf was white is...!

Japanese: ‥‥これが、それを立証する 致命的な証拠ですっ！
English: Here's the definitive piece of evidence that proves it!

Japanese: それが‥‥ 致命的な証拠、なのですか？
English: That...? That's your definitive piece of evidence?

Japanese: ええ！ ‥‥私の考えが正しければ。
English: Yes! ...If my thinking is correct.

Japanese: フッ‥‥それでナゾは解けた。 コタエはカンタンだ。
English: Well then. We have one mystery solved! The answer is simple.

Japanese: 弁護人の考えは正しくなかった。 ‥‥それだけのコト。
English: Ms. Fey's thinking is wrong. ...That's all there is to it.
Huh?

Japanese: たのむ。ソイツを引っこめてくれ。 オレまでミジメになっちまうぜ。
English: Please, Kitten. You've got to do something... You're killing me over here.

Japanese: 弁護人には、致命的な ペナルティを与えます。
English: Ms. Fey, I've got a definitive penalty to reward you for your definitive piece of evidence.

Japanese: （し‥‥しまった‥‥！）
English: (Well, that wasn't what I was expecting...)

Japanese: そもそも‥‥立証など できるのですかな？
English: Well... Do you have evidence or not?

Japanese: 証人が、このマフラーの色を 白だとカンちがいした理由など‥‥
English: Perhaps you can tell us about why the witness thought the scarf was white, eh...

Japanese: ‥‥証人。 このメモを見たことは？
English: ...Witness. Have you ever seen this note?

Japanese: ぞ‥‥存じ上げませんわ‥‥
English: N-Note...? I, err... No, never...

Japanese: ゴクヒ資料ですからな。 知らなくて当然でしょう。
English: It's top-secret evidence. There's no reason that you would have.

Japanese: ‥‥それはどうでしょうか‥‥
English: Hmm, I wonder about that...

Japanese: な、なんですの‥‥？
English: Wh-What do you mean...?

Japanese: このメモの中に、尾並田さんの 指示が書かれているんです。
English: This note shows Mr. Fawles's instructions to the victim regarding their meeting.

Japanese: 《目印に、白のマフラー》‥‥と。
English: It says... "Wear white scarf for identification."

Japanese: 白い‥‥マフラー‥‥‥？ ああああああっ！
English: White... Scarf...? AAAAAAH!!!

Japanese: 証人！　あなたは、このメモの 内容を知っていたのです！
English: Witness! You KNEW what this note said!

Japanese: それ以外に、マフラーの色を カンちがいできる方法は、ない！
English: There's no other possible reason for you to mistake the scarf's color!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、尾並田さんは、 美柳　勇希さんに要求しました。
English: ...It's true. Mr. Fawles did give instructions to Valerie Hawthorne about their meeting.

Japanese: 《白いマフラーを用意するように》 被害者のメモに書かれています。
English: The note that she left said: "Wear white scarf for identification."

Japanese: それが、何か‥‥？
English: Yes, what about it...?

Japanese: “何か”じゃありません！ あきらかにヘンじゃないですか！
English: Isn't it obvious!? Doesn't it strike you as odd!?

Japanese: そのメモの内容を‥‥、どうして この証人が知っているのですか！
English: That note was a secret piece of evidence! How is it that the witness knows about it!?

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！
静粛に！　静粛に！ それは‥‥やや不自然ぎみですな。
English: Order! Order! Hmm... It is odd, isn't it?

Japanese: まさか、御剣検事くん。
English: Well, Mr. Edgeworth!?

Japanese: 証人に、みだりにゴクヒ資料を 見せているワケでは‥‥？
English: Did you show top-secret evidence to the witness...?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥さすがに、 そのような許可はしていない。
English: Y-Your Honor... I certainly never gave anyone permission to do such a thing.

Japanese: それならば！　この証人が メモの内容を知っているはずが‥‥
English: In that case, the only way the witness could know about the note would b--

Japanese: お、お待ちくださいなっ！
English: J-Just a moment!

Japanese: わたし‥‥そんなメモなど、 もちろん知りませんわ！
English: Of course... Of course I didn't know about the note!

Japanese: ただ‥‥事件があったとき、 この目で拝見いたしましたの。
English: It's just... I saw it when it happened. ...With my own eyes.

Japanese: 被害者のかたが、ハンニンと お会いになったとき‥‥
English: When the victim went to see the killer...

Japanese: ご自分のマフラーを、あのかたに お示しになったんです。
English: She used her own scarf to signal him.

Japanese: だから、わたし‥‥白いマフラーが 目印なんだと思いましたの。
English: That's why... That's why I assumed that the signal was a white scarf.

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥それなら、 スジは通りますかな。
English: Hmmm... I suppose that makes sense.

Japanese: （どんなスジも通すワケには 行かないわよ、千尋！）
English: (Come on, Mia! You can't let her weasel out of it that easily!)

Japanese: ‥‥証人！　悪いけど、 あなたの主張は通らないわ。
English: ...Witness! Sorry, but your explanation isn't going to work here.

Japanese: ど、どうしてですの！ だってわたし、拝見しましたのに！
English: B-But why!? It-It's true! That's what I saw!

Japanese: 被害者が、現場で白いマフラーを 見せられるハズ、ありません！
English: Impossible. There's no way you could have seen the victim use a white scarf to signal him!

Japanese: ‥‥これが、それを立証する 致命的な証拠ですっ！
English: Here's the definitive piece of evidence that proves it!

Japanese: それが‥‥ 致命的な証拠、なのですか？
English: That...? That's your definitive piece of evidence?

Japanese: ええ！ ‥‥私の考えが正しければ。
English: Yes! ...If my thinking is correct.

Japanese: フッ‥‥それでナゾは解けた。 コタエはカンタンだ。
English: Well then. We have one mystery solved! The answer is simple.

Japanese: 弁護人の考えは正しくなかった。 ‥‥それだけのコト。
English: Ms. Fey's thinking is wrong. ...That's all there is to it.
Huh?

Japanese: たのむ。ソイツを引っこめてくれ。 オレまでミジメになっちまうぜ。
English: Please, Kitten. You've got to do something... You're killing me over here.

Japanese: 弁護人には、致命的な ペナルティを与えます。
English: Ms. Fey, I've got a definitive penalty to reward you for your definitive piece of evidence.

Japanese: （し‥‥しまった‥‥！）
English: (Well, that wasn't what I was expecting...)

Japanese: やっと信じていただけましたか？ わたしの申し上げたこと‥‥。
English: Well? ...Do you finally believe me?

Japanese: （こんなところで、モタモタ してる場合じゃないわ‥‥！）
English: (Come on, Mia! You're running out of time...!)

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは、さっきから 何度も証言していますね。
English: You've said several times in your testimony that the victim was wearing a scarf...

Japanese: 被害者のマフラーの色は 《白》だった、と。
English: ...A white scarf, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。それが どうかいたしまして‥‥？
English: Yes... Is something wrong...?

Japanese: それは、こっちが聞きたいわね。 ‥‥無久井　里子さん。
English: That's what I'd like to know. ...Ms. Foster.

Japanese: ごらんなさい。 これが、被害者のマフラーです！
English: Have a look at this. It's the victim's scarf!

Japanese: この色を、どう表現するかは 人それぞれでしょう。‥‥しかし！
English: I'm sure people would disagree about what to call this color. ...However!

Japanese: 決して《白》ではない！
English: It certainly is not white!

Japanese: あなたは、このメモの内容を 知っていたのです！
English: You KNEW what the note said!

Japanese: メモの‥‥内容‥‥‥？ ああああああっ！
English: She knew... the contents of the note...? N-NOOOOOOOO!

Japanese: ‥‥《目印に、白のマフラー》
English: It says, "Wear white scarf for identification."

Japanese: だからこそ！　マフラーの色を 《白》と表現してしまったのです！
English: That's the reason why you said it was white!
Ugh... Unn... Urgh...!

Japanese: どうなんですか、証人！
English: Well, Ms. Foster!?

Japanese: いやああああああああああああッ！
English: NOOOOOOOOO!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ 御剣くん！　どういうことですか！
English: Order! Order! Order! Mr. Edgeworth! I'm waiting for an explanation!

Japanese: このメモの内容が、外部に もれるなど、考えられないッ！
English: I'm quite sure this note wasn't leaked to the public!

Japanese: しかし‥‥この証人は、 メモの内容を知っていました！
English: And yet...! This witness knew exactly what the note said!

Japanese: その内容を知っていた人物は、 かぎられています。
English: At the time of the murder, the number of people that knew were quite... limited.

Japanese: 尾並田　美散と‥‥、メモを書いた 美柳　勇希本人‥‥
English: Terry Fawles is one. The person who wrote the note, Valerie Hawthorne, is another.

Japanese: そして‥‥もうひとり。
English: And finally... One more person.

Japanese: も‥‥“もうひとり”？
English: Did you say... "One more person"?

Japanese: そう。‥‥本来ならば “あり得ない人物”‥‥
English: That's right... A person that no one would have suspected...

Japanese: アンタも気づいたようだな？ コネコちゃん‥‥
English: Have you figured it out, Kitten?

Japanese: ‥‥はい！
English: Yup!

Japanese: 被害者のメモの内容を知っていた、 第３の人物。‥‥それはっ！
English: The third person that knew the contents of the note was...!

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと待つんだ、 コネコちゃん。
English: ...Hang on there, Kitten.

Japanese: な、なんですか！
English: What is it, Mr. Armando?

Japanese: コーヒー牛乳、おごってやるぜ。 ‥‥とびっきり、甘いヤツだ。
English: Here. I made some special café con leche for you... I put in plenty of sugar.

Japanese: さめないうちに、飲んじゃいな。
English: Drink it up before it gets cold. Don't be shy.

Japanese: あの‥‥。 どういうことですか？
English: Umm... Why are you doing this?

Japanese: あったかいのを飲んで、 もう一度、考えなおすんだ。
English: Your brain needs stimulation. Drink some of this and then think it over again.

Japanese: そんなの、提出したら‥‥ おジイちゃんに怒られちゃうぜ。
English: Listen. Keep messing up like that and you're going to get the judge mad.

Japanese: コラァ！　弁護人！
English: Ms. Fey!

Japanese: おいしそうにコーヒー牛乳を 飲まないようにッ！
English: Don't just stand there casually drinking coffee!

Japanese: （ううう‥‥。 ケッキョク怒られた‥‥）
English: (Oops... I think it's too late...)

Japanese: さ、裁判長！　本当に‥‥ もうひとり、いたんです！
English: B-But Your Honor...! Th-There is one more person!

Japanese: 尾並田さんと被害者の他に、 メモの内容を知っていた人物が！
English: There is a person besides Mr. Fawles and the victim that knew what the note said.

Japanese: ‥‥それは、この人物です。 美柳　ちなみさん。
English: And that person is... Dahlia Hawthorne!

Japanese: “美柳　ちなみ”‥‥？ 聞いたことありませんが。
English: Dahlia Hawthorne...? I've never heard that name before.

Japanese: 被害者のメモを見てください。 ‥‥こう書かれています。
English: Look at the victim's note. This is what it says...

Japanese: 《ちなみに相談。今度こそ伝える》 ‥‥彼女の名前です。
English: "Talk to Dahlia. Tell her this time..." There is her name, right there.

Japanese: い、いったい‥‥どなたですか！ 美柳　ちなみさん、というのは‥‥
English: Wh-What's this... So who is this person!? This... Dahlia Hawthorne...

Japanese: フッ‥‥
English: *sigh*...

Japanese: こともあろうに、 死者を引き出してくるとは、な。
English: Ms. Fey must be desperate if she's trying to bring the dead back to life.

Japanese: ししゃ‥‥？
English: The d-dead...?

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみは‥‥ 被害者・美柳　勇希の妹にあたる。
English: Dahlia Hawthorne was the victim's deceased younger sister.

Japanese: 今から５年前‥‥ある犯罪に 巻きこまれて、亡くなった。
English: She was killed in a crime 5 years ago.

Japanese: “ある犯罪”‥‥？　ま、まさか！
English: Killed in a crime...? Y-You don't mean...!?

Japanese: 誘拐されて‥‥殺害された。 被告人・尾並田　美散によって！
English: Yes. She was kidnapped... And killed. By Terry Fawles!

Japanese: “殺害された”‥‥ 本当にそうなのでしょうか？
English: You said she was "killed"... But was she really?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだと‥‥？
English: What are you implying...?

Japanese: たしかに《美柳　ちなみ》は、 死んだことになっています。
English: Of course, people thought she had died 5 years ago...

Japanese: ５年前、おぼろ橋から転落して‥‥ 吾童川の急流に飲みこまれて。
English: ...when she fell off of Dusky Bridge and was lost in the Eagle River.

Japanese: しかし！　カンジンの死体は 発見されていないのです！
English: However! Her corpse was never found!

Japanese: 死亡認定の手つづきは、 すでに５年前に済んでいる！
English: She was declared legally dead 5 years ago!

Japanese: 法律的には、美柳　ちなみは すでに、死者なのだ‥‥
English: As far as the law is concerned, Dahlia Hawthorne is officially dead.

Japanese: しかし、死体が見つかって いないのは、ジジツです！
English: But the fact remains that her body was never recovered!

Japanese: ５年前‥‥。美柳　ちなみさんは、 １４才だったそうです。
English: Dahlia Hawthorne was 14 years old, 5 years ago.

Japanese: 生きていれば‥‥今年、１９才。
English: If she were still alive, she would be 19 now.

Japanese: ‥‥無久井　里子さん。
English: ...Melissa Foster.

Japanese: ちょうど、あなたぐらいの 年ごろですね‥‥。
English: I believe that's the same age you are!

Japanese: え‥‥っ！
まさか‥‥弁護人！ この証人が‥‥？
English: Even you couldn't...! Ms. Fey...! You're not saying...!

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。 弁護側は主張します。
English: But I am. That's precisely what I'm saying.

Japanese: その証言台に立っている “証人”は‥‥
English: This "witness" before us is the girl that was kidnapped and killed 5 years ago!

Japanese: ５年前の誘拐事件の中心人物、 美柳　ちなみである、と！
English: This girl is in fact Ms. Dahlia Hawthorne!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですとおおおおおおッ！
English: WHA...! WHAAAAAAT!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　どうやら、火事場に バクダンを放りこんじまったな。
English: Ha...! Nice work... That was like tossing a grenade into a 3-alarm fire!

Japanese: このまま終わったら‥‥
English: But unless you can tie all the loose ends together...

Japanese: ただのヒトさわがせな お姉さん、だぜ。
English: ...you're nothing but a hit-and-run arsonist.

Japanese: わ、わかってます！
English: I... I understand.

Japanese: （彼女の正体が暴ければ‥‥ かならず、状況は変わるわ！）
English: (If I can expose her true nature, I can turn this whole case on its head!)

Japanese: （今度は、あの検事さんが あわてふためく番よ‥‥！）
English: (Now is my chance to make Mr. Edgeworth squirm...!)

Japanese: ぬ‥‥ぬううううう‥‥
English: Hmm... Hmm... Hmmm...

Japanese: 証人！　あなたは‥‥ いったい、ダレなのですかッ！
English: Witness! Just who are you anyway!?

Japanese: わたし‥‥わたしは‥‥ I... I... I'm...

English: I didn't think it'd come to this.

Japanese: もう‥‥そのへんで いいだろう、証人。
English: That's enough... You don't have to say anymore, witness.

Japanese: はい。わかりましたわ。
English: Yes. I understand.

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか！ 御剣検事くん‥‥
English: Wh-What! Mr. Edgeworth! Explain yourself!

Japanese: 裁判長。‥‥正直に告白しよう。
English: Your Honor. I have an admission to make.

Japanese: 弁護側がここまで食い下がるとは ‥‥予想外だった。
English: I honestly never thought the defense would pursue the matter this far...

Japanese: 御剣検事！ ま、まさか、あなたは‥‥
English: You don't...! You don't mean...!

Japanese: ‥‥検事局を 見くびってもらっては困る。
English: ...Yes. The prosecutor's office isn't filled with fools, you know.

Japanese: 身元のハッキリしない証人など、 この私がユルすハズがない。
English: Naturally, we conduct full background checks on all of our witnesses.

Japanese: な、なんですって‥‥？
English: Wh-What did he say...?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　どうやら‥‥ ボウヤは、知ってやがったのさ。
English: Ha...! It looks like the kid knew.

Japanese: 彼女の正体を‥‥最初っから、な。
English: He knew her true identity from the get-go.

Japanese: そんな‥‥！ じゃあ、どうして！
English: No way...! But then why...!

Japanese: アンタが暴かなければ、 触れないつもりだったんだろう。
English: If you hadn't revealed her secret, he wasn't going to say anything about it.

Japanese: 必要なのは、《証言》だった。 それが、取引ってヤツさ。
English: All he wanted was her testimony, so he made a little trade.

Japanese: ‥‥紹介しよう。
English: Let me introduce you to...

Japanese: 被害者の妹にあたる‥‥ 美柳　ちなみさん、だ。
English: ...the victim's younger sister, Ms. Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: し‥‥しかし！　５年前に 亡くなったのでは‥‥？
English: But... But... I thought she died 5 years ago...?

Japanese: われわれもそう考えていた。 しかし‥‥そうではなかった。
English: We thought so as well... But... Well, as you can see...

Japanese: いったい、なぜ！　５年間も 身をかくしていたのですか！
English: Why!? Why did she hide her identity for 5 years!?

Japanese: ‥‥それは、今回の事件とは 関係のないことだ。
English: That has nothing to do with the current case.

Japanese: 彼女は、たまたま 事件を目撃しただけ‥‥
English: She was merely an accidental witness to a crime...

Japanese: “たまたま”‥‥？ そんなはず、ないでしょう！
English: Accidental...? I don't believe that for a minute!

Japanese: あなたは、５年間も 《被害者》の仮面をかぶっていた！
English: For the last 5 years, she's been playing the role of victim!

Japanese: そして‥‥今回も！　殺人現場で 疑わしい行動をとっています！
English: And now we find her acting suspiciously at another murder scene!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人のウスっぺらい たくらみは、見えている‥‥
English: ...Really, Ms. Fey! Your strategy is clearly obvious.

Japanese: おおかた、被告人の無罪を 勝ちとるために‥‥
English: You're trying to pin your client's crime on an innocent witness in order to win...

Japanese: この証人に、無実の罪を 着せようというのだろう。
English: ...At any cost.

Japanese: ‥‥なんですって‥‥！
English: H-How dare you!

Japanese: しかし‥‥考えていただきたい。 今から５年前、彼女は‥‥
English: Please, let us take a moment to think.

Japanese: 誘拐され、殺害されかけた ツラい過去を抱えているのだ。
English: Five years ago, this girl was kidnapped and nearly killed.

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: そして、何より‥‥
English: But even worse than that... 5 years later...

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみは、被害者の たったひとりの妹なのだ。
English: ...Dahlia Hawthorne lost something much more precious. Her big sister.

Japanese: りっぱな刑事である姉を 殺害する理由など、ないッ！
English: Ms. Fey must be insane to even suggest that she murdered her.

Japanese: うううううッ！
English: Whaaaaat!?

Japanese: ‥‥検察側の主張は、 もっともだと考えます。
English: ...I'm inclined to agree with the prosecutor's logic.

Japanese: 弁護人！　あなたは 立証できると言うのですかッ！
English: Ms. Fey! Do you have any evidence to back up your assertion!?

Japanese: この、キノドクな証人が、 お姉さまを殺害した理由をッ！
English: What possible reason would this witness have for killing her beloved sister!?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥‥
English: W-Well you see...

Japanese: （追いつめたつもりが‥‥ いつのまにか、逆転されてる！）
English: (I thought I was winning, but somehow he's turned it around on me!)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
目をさましてやろうか、 コネコちゃん‥‥
English: I think you need a little push in the right direction, Kitten...

Japanese: ‥‥弁護側には、証人の動機を 立証する用意がありますっ！
English: The defense is prepared to present evidence supporting our claim!

Japanese: あ！　今のは、私じゃなくて、 このコーヒーのヒトが勝手に‥‥
English: Ah! That wasn't me! It was this guy... This crazy coffee addict...

Japanese: ヘタなハッタリは やめていただきたいな‥‥キミ。
English: I think we've heard enough empty threats from you, old man.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　ボウヤ。なぜ ハッタリだとわかる‥‥？
English: Ha...! What makes you think they're empty, boy?

Japanese: そこの弁護士が、イチバン あたふたしているからだッ！
English: Because your protégé looks like she's sweating bullets!

Japanese: え‥‥！ （そりゃ、あたふたするわよ！）
English: Ah...! (I AM sweating bullets!)

Japanese: ‥‥アンタは今、この状況を ピンチだと考えている。そうだろ？
English: ...You think you're in a tough spot, huh?

Japanese: あたりまえですっ！
English: Of course! Aren't I?

Japanese: ‥‥ちがう。 今は、ただ《勝負時》なんだ。
English: ...No. You've just arrived at the moment of truth, that's all.

Japanese: ピンチか、チャンスか？ 決めるのは、アンタ自身なのさ。
English: Whether you win or lose... That's up to you.

Japanese: （私自身が‥‥決める‥‥）
English: (Up to me...?)

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。若さゆえの暴走、か。 ほほえましいな‥‥
English: *sigh*... The rashness of youth. How charming.

Japanese: （私より若いくせに）
English: (This coming from someone younger than me!)

Japanese: それでは、弁護側の考えを うかがいましょう。
English: Now then, let's not waste any more time. ...Ms. Fey.

Japanese: この証人が‥‥実の姉である 美柳　勇希を殺害した“動機”は！
English: What motive would this witness have for murdering her own sister, Valerie Hawthorne?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだろうか。 今の、不吉なシロモノは？
English: ...What is this? Is this the defense's idea of a joke?

Japanese: イミがわかりません。 なんなのですか？　弁護人。
English: If so, I certainly don't get the punch line... Well? Ms. Fey?

Japanese: 今のは‥‥、その。 若さゆえの暴走ですっ！
English: Oh, that was... The rashness of youth!

Japanese: じ、自分で 認めてどうするんですかッ！
English: Th-The rashness of youth!? And what is your point in furthering such a stereotype!?

Japanese: あの証人は、５年間も 身をひそめてきた。
English: That witness stayed hidden for 5 years, Kitten.

Japanese: かならず、理由があるはずだ。 そして‥‥
English: There must be a good reason for that.

Japanese: 美柳　勇希が、その“理由”に からんでいるのさ‥‥
English: And somehow it must involve Valerie Hawthorne...

Japanese: （もう一度‥‥！　法廷記録の スミズミまで見るの、千尋！）
English: (OK, one more time, Mia! You gotta read the Court Record more carefully this time!)

Japanese: ‥‥尾並田さんの脱走が、 すべての始まりでした。
English: ...The story starts after Terry Fawles escaped.

Japanese: 彼に呼び出された勇希さんが 書いた、このメモ‥‥
English: He called Valerie and told her he wanted to meet. This is the note she left...

Japanese: こう書かれています。 『ちなみに相談。今度こそ伝える』
English: It says, "Talk to Dahlia. Tell her this time, the whole truth must come out."

Japanese: ‥‥美柳　ちなみは、勇希さんから 宣告されたのです。
English: ...Valerie Hawthorne gave Dahlia a warning.

Japanese: 《すべて、世間に公表する》 ‥‥と。
English: She told her she was going to reveal to the world the whole truth.
The whole truth...?

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみと被害者の間には、 大きなヒミツがあったのです。
English: There was a dangerously important secret between Valerie and Dahlia.

Japanese: だから、美柳　ちなみは 姉を殺害しなければならなかった！
English: That's the reason Dahlia felt she had to kill Valerie...

Japanese: その口を、永久にふさぐために！
English: To keep her mouth shut permanently!

Japanese: ‥‥アマいな、弁護士クン。 それでは、だれもナットクしない。
English: A terrific story, Ms. Fey! If you like fiction, that is.

Japanese: その“大きなヒミツ”とやらが、 いったい、なんだったのか‥‥？
English: Enlighten the court, Ms. Fey. What was this secret that was so important?

Japanese: それが立証できないかぎりは！
English: Where's your evidence!?

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみ、勇希、そして 尾並田　美散‥‥
English: Dahlia and Valerie Hawthorne, and Terry Fawles...

Japanese: ３人をつなぐ“大きなヒミツ”。 ‥‥１つしか、ねえぜ。
English: There's only one important secret that connects them all...

Japanese: ええ。‥‥わかっています。
English: Oh, yes... I know this secret.

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長！　弁護側は、 証言を要求します。
English: ...Your Honor! The defense would like to request further testimony.

Japanese: い、いったい‥‥なんの 証言ですかな？
English: Wh-What testimony?

Japanese: ５年前の誘拐事件について。 そこにコタエがあるはずです。
English: Regarding the kidnapping 5 years ago. We believe it will explain a lot of things.

Japanese: 美柳姉妹の、“大きなヒミツ” ‥‥その正体が！
English: Such as the nature of the important secret between the Hawthorne sisters!

Japanese: ぐっ‥‥！
English: Ugh...!

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 証言をおねがいしましょう。
English: ...Very well. I'll grant your request for further testimony.

Japanese: ツラい記憶でしょうが、 話していただけますね？
English: I know it will be painful for you, but can enlighten us once more, my little maple leaf?

Japanese: え、ええ。 もちろん、かまいませんわ‥‥
English: Y-Yes, I-I'll try. Mr. Judge...

Japanese: （いよいよエガオが キレイね、ちなみさん‥‥）
English: (Putting on the old charm one more time, Dahlia?)

Japanese: （‥‥でも。 それも、これが最後よ！）
English: (But this will be the last time you hide behind your womanly wiles!)

Japanese: ～５年前の事件～
English: -- 5 Years Ago --

Japanese: ５年前‥‥わたし、尾並田さまに 誘拐されましたの。
English: Five years ago, I was kidnapped by Mr. Fawles.

Japanese: 身代金は、ダイヤの原石でした。 おぼろ橋の上で、お姉さまは‥‥
English: The ransom price was a raw diamond. My sister, Valerie, brought it to the bridge...

Japanese: 取り引きが終わったとき、 尾並田さまの右腕を撃ちましたの！
English: After she made the exchange, she shot Mr. Fawles in the arm!

Japanese: 尾並田さまは、わたしを殺すために 背中を押して、つき落としました！
English: That's when Mr. Fawles tried to kill me by shoving me off the bridge from behind!

Japanese: わたし‥‥美柳家のムスメでいる のが、コワくなってしまって‥‥
English: I survived, but I was afraid I might be kidnapped again for my family's money...

Japanese: それ以来、身分をいつわって、 今まで生活してきたんです‥‥。
English: So I decided to change my identity and start a new life...

Japanese: ぬうううううう‥‥
English: Hmmm...

Japanese: ‥‥誘拐事件が、彼女のココロに 大きなキズを残したのだ。
English: ...The kidnapping left her emotionally scarred.

Japanese: 美柳家を去り、姉の協力を得て 第二の人生を歩んでいた。
English: With her sister's help, she left the Hawthorne family and started all over again.

Japanese: それなのに‥‥その姉を、殺害？ ‥‥考えられぬッ！
English: And we're to believe after all that, she murdered her sister? Preposterous!

Japanese: 事情は、わかりました。 ‥‥弁護人。
English: Thank you, Mr. Edgeworth. ...Ms. Fey?

Japanese: は、はい。
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: お聞きのとおり、証人は今もなお、 ココロにキズを負っています。
English: As you've heard, the witness is still traumatized from the kidnapping.

Japanese: ‥‥くれぐれも、尋問には 気をつけてもらいたいですね。
English: ...I'll ask you again to be extremely gentle in your cross-examination.

Japanese: （‥‥また、先手を 打たれてしまった‥‥）
English: (...Mr. Edgeworth got the jump on me again...)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　ここまで来たら、 カケヒキなんざ、ねじふせろ。
English: Ha...! If we're not allowed to fight, then let's twist some arms.

Japanese: ‥‥いいか。オレたちには１つ、 切り札となる情報があるんだぜ。
English: ...Listen up. We've still got that info... That ace up our sleeve.

Japanese: ‥‥なんですか！
English: ...What info?

Japanese: 決まってるじゃねえか。 オレたちは、知っている。
English: Come on, Kitten. Don't say you've forgotten already.

Japanese: ５年前の《誘拐事件》が‥‥ すべて、狂言だったことを！
English: The fact that the kidnapping 5 years ago was staged!

Japanese: （《狂言誘拐》‥‥。控え室で、 尾並田さんは言っていた）
English: (That's right, it was a fake kidnapping! Terry Fawles told us that in the lobby.)

Japanese: アイツの言うコト、なんでもやる。 ちなみに言われたコトだったら‥‥
English: I do anything she says. Anything Dahlia says...

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥５年前の 誘拐を計画したのは‥‥？
English: What you're saying is that the kidnapping 5 years ago was planned by...

Japanese: オレと、ちなみ。 ‥‥勇希も、仲間だった。
English: Yeah. Me and Dahlia... ...And Valerie, too.

Japanese: （そう‥‥この《狂言誘拐》を 立証するの、千尋！）
English: (Yes! That's it! The fake kidnapping is your best shot, Mia!)

Japanese: （それこそが、彼女の “ヒミツ”なんだから‥‥）
English: (That's her secret!)

Japanese: ～５年前の事件～
English: -- 5 Years Ago --

Japanese: ５年前‥‥わたし、尾並田さまに 誘拐されましたの。
English: Five years ago, I was kidnapped by Mr. Fawles.

Japanese: 身代金は、ダイヤの原石でした。 おぼろ橋の上で、お姉さまは‥‥
English: The ransom price was a raw diamond. My sister, Valerie, brought it to the bridge...

Japanese: 取り引きが終わったとき、 尾並田さまの右腕を撃ちましたの！
English: After she made the exchange, she shot Mr. Fawles in the arm!

Japanese: 尾並田さまは、わたしを殺すために 背中を押して、つき落としました！
English: That's when Mr. Fawles tried to kill me by shoving me off the bridge from behind!

Japanese: わたし‥‥美柳家のムスメでいる のが、コワくなってしまって‥‥
English: I survived, but I was afraid I might be kidnapped again for my family's money...

Japanese: それ以来、身分をいつわって、 今まで生活してきたんです‥‥。
English: So I decided to change my identity and start a new life...

Japanese: あの証人にとっても、まさに ここが勝負どころさ。
English: It's the moment of truth for this witness, too.

Japanese: ５年前の事件が《狂言誘拐》 だったこと‥‥
English: Once the truth about this staged kidnapping comes out...

Japanese: ソイツが立証されたとき‥‥ あの子の正体は、すべて暴かれる！
English: Everyone in the court will know how much of a Jezebel she really is!

Japanese: （仕組まれた誘拐事件‥‥ かならず立証してみせるわ！）
English: (I've just got to prove that kidnapping was a hoax!)

Japanese: 尾並田さんとあなたは‥‥ 知り合いだったのですか？
English: Did you and Mr. Fawles have a relationship?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。 わたし、お勉強を‥‥
English: ...Y-Yes. As a tutor...

Japanese: 教えていたんですか？ 尾並田さんに。
English: You were tutoring him? Mr. Fawles?

Japanese: と、とんでもありません！ 教えていただいておりましたの。
English: N-No, of course not! Don't be ridiculous!

Japanese: 家庭教師だったんです。 尾並田　美散さまは‥‥
English: Mr. Fawles came to the house to tutor ME!

Japanese: （‥‥そりゃそうね。５年前、 彼女は１４才だったんだから）
English: (That makes sense... 5 years ago, she was only 14.)

Japanese: 美柳家に出入りしているうちに、 計画を思いついたのだろう。
English: He probably came up with the kidnapping plan during that time.

Japanese: 資産家の宝石商だ。 いくらでもカネがとれる‥‥
English: The Hawthornes are in the jewelry trade and are quite wealthy, you see.

Japanese: ぬうううううう‥‥ アタマのいいオトコですねえ。
English: Hmm... Quite the clever fellow, that Mr. Fawles.

Japanese: （“アタマのいいオトコ”か‥‥）
English: (Did I hear him right!? Did he just call Mr. Fawles a clever fellow?)

Japanese: たしか‥‥ダイヤは、 ２億円の価値があった、とか。
English: I heard the diamond is valued in the neighboorhood of 2 million dollars.

Japanese: ににに、におくえん！
English: 2 million dollars!?

Japanese: カットする前のダイヤの原石で、 おトウフぐらいの大きさでしたわ。
English: It was still uncut, so it was about the size of a pint of milk.

Japanese: ぬうううううう‥‥！
English: HMMMMM!!!

Japanese: ２億円のおトウフですか‥‥。 私はどうかと思いますな！
English: A 2 million dollar pint of milk... I don't know what to think about that!

Japanese: 犯人の要求によって、取引相手には 姉の勇希さんが指定された。
English: The defendant demanded that her sister, Valerie, make the exchange.

Japanese: もちろん、刑事としてではなく‥‥ あくまでも、個人として。
English: Not as a detective, of course, but as an individual.

Japanese: ところで‥‥
English: By the way, I want to ask you, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: どうして、あんな山の中で 取り引きをしたのでしょうか。
English: Why do you think he wanted to make the exchange up there on that mountain?

Japanese: 逃げにくそうだし、 犯人に不利に見えますが‥‥
English: If he ever got surrounded, it would be hard to escape...

Japanese: 誘拐犯の考えることは、１つ。 警察の介入だ。
English: There's one thing a kidnapper wants to prevent, and that's police involvement.

Japanese: さみしい山の中ならば、 尾行の存在がわかりやすい。
English: In a place like that, it would be easy to tell if he was being followed.

Japanese: 犯人は、山の入り口で 安全をたしかめることができる。
English: With only one entrance to the mountain, he was ensuring his safety.

Japanese: かしこいかたでしたのね。 尾並田さま‥‥
English: What a wickedly clever man that Mr. Fawles is...

Japanese: （自分で考えたクセに‥‥）
English: (Yeah, right. It was all YOUR plan...)

Japanese: とにかく、おぼろ橋の上で お姉さまは‥‥
English: Anyway, Valerie brought the diamond to the mountain and...

Japanese: あなたが、人質にとられているのに ‥‥キケンなことをしましたね。
English: That was a dangerous thing to do considering you were being held hostage.

Japanese: でも‥‥‥おかげでわたし、 助かったのですよ。
English: Yes, but... Actually, that saved my life.

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: 尾並田さま、右手にナイフを お持ちになっていて‥‥
English: You see, Mr. Fawles was holding a knife in his right hand...

Japanese: わたし‥‥ハッキリ わかりましたの。
English: Somehow, I just KNEW he was going to use it.

Japanese: このかたは、わたしを お殺しになるつもりだ、って。
English: I knew he was going to use that knife to kill me!

Japanese: だから、お姉さまは ピストルをお撃ちになったのです！
English: That's why my sister shot him! It was to save me!

Japanese: そのときのこと‥‥もっと 詳しく聞かせてください！
English: I'd like to hear more about what happened right at that moment!

Japanese: 右腕を撃たれて‥‥尾並田さまは わたしの腕を、おはなしに‥‥
English: Well, when Mr. Fawles was shot in the right arm, he let go of me...

Japanese: ムチュウでした。 逃げようとおもった、そのとき‥‥
English: I-I was dazed. I turned to try and run away, but Mr. Fawles turned to grab me as well...

Japanese: 尾並田さまと目が合いました。 殺意にみちた、おそろしい目‥‥
English: As I ran past, he and I locked eyes for a second, and he gave me a large, bloodthirsty grin.

Japanese: さ、殺意ィィ‥‥ッ！
English: B-Bloodthirsty grin... Oooh...

Japanese: そして、次の瞬間‥‥
English: And in the next instant...

Japanese: ‥‥川の水深は６メートルほど。 流れはかなり早い‥‥
English: I advise the court to remember that the river is 18 feet deep and incredibly swift...

Japanese: わたし、およぐのはトクイでした。 でも、気を失ってしまって‥‥
English: I-I was a strong swimmer, but I was knocked out...

Japanese: 気がついたら、見たこともない 山の中に流れついていたんです。
English: When I came to, I had been carried away by the river to a strange place.

Japanese: わたし‥‥今でも 忘れられません。
English: I'll never forget that day.

Japanese: コワれかけた橋の上で‥‥ 逃げ道もなくて。
English: The crumbling bridge, nowhere to run...

Japanese: 背中を‥‥ほんの、ひと押し。 それだけでしたわ。
English: Then just one little shove from behind. That was it.

Japanese: お姉さまが止める間もなく、 ‥‥川の中へ‥‥
English: Before my sister could catch me... ...I fell into the river.

Japanese: ‥‥それで、 名乗り出なかったのですか。
English: And that's why you hid your identity?

Japanese: ええ‥‥。 お姉さまにだけ、連絡をとって。
English: Yes... I only told my sister.

Japanese: 被害者の勇希さん、ですな。
English: Valerie Hawthorne, eh.

Japanese: わたしのことを知っていたのは、 お姉さまだけでした。
English: Yes, she's the only one who knew about me.

Japanese: 法律的には‥‥すでに５年前。 この証人は死んでいるのだ。
English: Meanwhile, legally, this witness has been deceased for 5 years.

Japanese: わたし‥‥もう、あんな目に あいたくなくて‥‥
English: I... I didn't ever want something like that to happen to me again.

Japanese: それが、《無久井　里子》 というわけですね？
English: And that new identity was "Melissa Foster", right?

Japanese: 必要な書類は、お姉さまに 用意していただきました。
English: Yes, my sister helped me get the official paperwork taken care of.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥）
English: (That makes sense...)

Japanese: （公務員の協力がなければ、 ムリな話ね‥‥）
English: (Without an insider's help, doing all of the paperwork would've been impossible.)

Japanese: わたしには、お姉さましか たよれるヒトがいなかったんです。
English: She was the only person left in the world I could count on.

Japanese: そんなお姉さまを こ、殺すなんて‥‥ムリですわ！
English: And you... You think I k-k-killed her... There's no way I could!

Japanese: ぬふううううう‥‥！
English: Hmmmmm...!

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、尾並田　美散に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Terry Fawles...

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!
